items.title: Streamline Suno Model Dropdown by Removing Pro Tag in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: The Pro tag has been removed from the Suno model dropdown options, simplifying the interface for all users. This change eliminates visual clutter, as all AceDataCloud users have access to every Suno model without tier distinctions. The update involved modifications to the TypeSelector component and related styles, resulting in a cleaner presentation of model options.

items.title: Polish UI Elements for Consistency in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update enhances the UI by unifying section headers and adjusting the font size of the lyrics textarea. The Vocal Gender block's margin has been refined for better alignment, and the Persona section title has been changed to match the styling of other headings. Additionally, the lyrics textarea font size has been increased for improved readability without overwhelming the layout.

items.title: Streamline Task Services with New Base Operator in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update successfully refactors 14 task services by migrating them to a shared base operator and factory, resulting in a significant reduction of over 3,200 lines of code. Key improvements include the introduction of generic abstractions that eliminate redundant boilerplate, enhancing maintainability and performance. All previous bugs have been addressed, ensuring a smooth transition to the new structure.

items.title: Streamline Token Initialization in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the token-dependent initialization process in the chat component by moving all related operations into a single watcher. This change eliminates race conditions that previously caused issues during cold logins, ensuring that conversations load correctly without errors. The previous retry mechanism has been removed, simplifying the code and improving performance.

items.title: Suno UI Enhancements for a Sleeker Look in Nexior ✨
items.summary: This update refines the Suno configuration panel by adjusting the sizing of various UI components for better visual consistency. Key changes include shrinking the font size of the lyrics textarea, restoring the visibility of the Manage Voices button, and aligning the sizes of input elements. These improvements enhance the overall user experience without altering any existing functionality.

items.title: Enhance Google Skills with GWS CLI Support in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces optional Google Workspace CLI (`gws`) documentation for Gmail, Calendar, and Drive skills, optimizing specific operations for better user experience. Additionally, it removes misleading `pip install` hints from Tencent skills, clarifying that dependencies are preinstalled in the sandbox environment. These changes aim to streamline interactions and improve error messaging for users.

items.title: Align Suno Service Costs with API Billing in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a discrepancy between the displayed cost on Nexior's Suno panel and the actual API billing. The service cost file has been updated to reflect the correct pricing, ensuring users see accurate consumption indicators. Additionally, model conditions have been normalized for consistency with the API cost file.

items.title: Refactor task operators to BaseTaskOperator in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update migrates 12 task operators to the new `BaseTaskOperator` structure, enhancing code consistency and maintainability. The migration preserves existing behavior and reduces the codebase by over 1,300 lines. Key operators like `seedance`, `sora`, and `veo` have been successfully transitioned, ensuring a smoother integration moving forward.

items.title: Revamp Suno Mode Switcher with el-tabs in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: The Suno page's left config panel has been updated to replace the custom mode buttons with an `el-tabs` component for switching between Simple and Custom modes. This change enhances visual consistency with Midjourney's interface and simplifies styling by utilizing Element Plus tokens. The new setup ensures that only relevant fields are displayed for each mode, improving user experience.

items.title: Fix conversation loading issue in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a race condition that prevented conversations from displaying in the chat panel after a fresh login. A new watcher on the credential token has been added to ensure that conversations are fetched once the token is available, improving the user experience. The fix has been tested across multiple chat groups to ensure consistent functionality.

items.title: Improve song list appearance with line clamp in Nexior 🎶
items.summary: This update implements a CSS-only fix to limit song descriptions in the Suno song list to two lines, enhancing the overall appearance of the recent panel. By adding the `-webkit-line-clamp` property, long descriptions will now be truncated with ellipses, preventing clutter and improving readability. Other elements in the panel remain unchanged.

items.title: Revamped User Question Wizard for Enhanced UX in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: The user question card has been transformed into a sleek one-at-a-time wizard, improving clarity and user experience. Each question now features its own 'Other' option with an inline textarea, and navigation has been streamlined with clear progress indicators. This update not only enhances usability but also changes the output structure for better data handling.

items.title: Enhance Sandbox with Tencent Cloud SDKs and kubectl in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the `tencentcloud-sdk-python` and `kubectl` to the sandbox environment, addressing critical dependencies for several Tencent skills. The Dockerfile has been modified to include these tools, along with smoke checks to ensure their proper installation. As a result, skills relying on these packages will function correctly without runtime installation errors.

items.title: Fix skill directory display for model in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the response header for the `load_skill` function to display the correct skill directory as `$SKILL_DIR` instead of the worker pod path. This change prevents errors when the model attempts to access files that do not exist in the sandbox environment. Additionally, tests have been updated to ensure the correct placeholder is used and that the worker path is not leaked.

items.title: Streamline Copilot PR Management in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the scheduled research workflow by implementing automatic closure of stale Copilot PRs, addressing a backlog of 11 open PRs. New steps have been added to close lingering drafts before starting a new cycle and to handle failures during the merge process. This ensures that only one open Copilot PR exists at any time, improving workflow efficiency.

items.title: Fix auto-merge for Sync-from-Docs workflow in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves multiple bugs in the Sync-from-Docs workflow that caused PRs to remain in draft status and accumulate over time. Key changes include improved cleanup of stale PRs, enhanced binding of PRs to issues, and a more reliable completion signal. The adjustments aim to streamline the merging process and prevent silent failures, ensuring a smoother workflow experience.

items.title: Fix auto-merge for Sync-from-Docs workflow in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses multiple bugs in the Sync-from-Docs workflow that caused PRs to remain in draft status and accumulate over time. Key improvements include enhanced cleanup of stale PRs, better binding of PRs to issues, and a more reliable completion signal. With these changes, every docs update will now lead to a clear outcome, reducing backlog and improving efficiency.

items.title: Fix Sync-from-Docs Auto-Merge Issues in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves multiple bugs in the Sync-from-Docs workflow that caused PRs to accumulate without merging. Key changes include improved cleanup of stale PRs, enhanced binding of PRs to issues, and a more reliable completion signal. The adjustments aim to streamline the process, ensuring that each docs update dispatch results in a clear outcome.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates from zh-CN in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This PR introduces automated translation updates for internationalization (i18n) based on changes from the zh-CN source. All relevant cache files have been renamed to reflect these updates, ensuring that the application remains up-to-date with the latest translations. This enhancement will improve the user experience for Chinese-speaking users.

items.title: Scheduled Research Cycle Update in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the latest research cycle for May 5, 2026, with a comprehensive review of 20 candidates. Key decisions include holding two tokens, ELITE and OSAMA, while skipping nine due to red flags. The data source health is mostly stable, though some sources are degraded, impacting credibility checks.

items.title: Enhance Image Generation Options in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two new optional parameters for the image generation command in the CLI. Users can now specify the maximum number of sequential images and choose an optimization mode for prompts, enhancing the flexibility and performance of image generation tasks. These changes align the CLI with the latest API updates, ensuring a smoother user experience.

items.title: Documentation Update for Overview Service in M C Ps 📚
items.summary: This update introduces a documentation-only change for the overview service, adding a new `<Note title="Tip">` section to the `mcp/overview.mdx` file across 18 language variants. There are no changes to the OpenAPI specifications or the directory structure, ensuring a seamless sync process.

items.title: Enhance MCP Servers Section with Helpful Tip in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a new tip note to the MCP Servers section of the README, reflecting the recent addition in the upstream documentation. The note clarifies the purpose of the MCP servers, emphasizing their role in facilitating information exchange among AI assistants. This enhancement aims to improve user understanding and engagement with the MCP servers.

items.title: Centralize Webhook Callback URL in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the webhook callback URL by moving it from 20 hard-coded instances into a single helper function, `getWebhookCallbackUrl()`, located in `src/constants/endpoint.ts`. This change simplifies future migrations to different webhook hosts and ensures consistency across the codebase, with no behavior changes expected. All affected files have been updated to use the new helper function, maintaining the same runtime payload.

items.title: Centralize Surface Checks with New Helpers in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the handling of Capacitor surface checks by introducing new utility functions in `src/utils/surface.ts`. The previous checks, which were duplicated across seven locations, are now centralized, improving maintainability and readability. The changes ensure no behavioral alterations while streamlining the codebase.

items.title: Documentation Update: No SDK Changes Needed in S D K 🚀
items.summary: Recent updates to the documentation for the overview service have been reviewed and confirmed as non-breaking. Key changes include the addition of a note block and optional parameters in the OpenAPI files, but no new providers or endpoints were introduced. The SDK remains fully compatible with all existing features.

items.title: Ensure Fresh User Selection Reflected in Nexior 🔄
items.summary: This update addresses an issue where the application picker displayed stale balance information due to reliance on persisted data. The fix ensures that the latest values from the server are used, preserving user selection while reflecting accurate balances. Key changes include modifying the logic to return fresh application data instead of outdated persisted references.

items.title: Refine Connector Actions for Clarity in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update enhances the visual layout of the Connectors detail header by reducing the gap between the 'Refresh' and 'Disconnect' buttons from 8px to 4px, making them appear as a cohesive group. Additionally, the 'Disconnect' button has been changed to a 'danger' type to emphasize its destructive nature, aligning with user feedback for clearer action indicators.

items.title: Streamline Dependencies and Wallet Adapters in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update drops three unused dependencies, including React and PSL, which had zero imports in the source code. Additionally, it switches from a catch-all Solana wallet meta-package to individual adapters for Phantom, Solflare, Nightly, and WalletConnect, resulting in a significant reduction of 367 packages in node_modules. This change enhances the project's efficiency and reduces potential vulnerabilities.

items.title: Enhance timestamp parsing for better API logging in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the timestamp parsing functionality in the API logging system. It allows the system to handle various timestamp formats, including epoch milliseconds and seconds, ensuring accurate logging of request durations for Kong-only proxy services. Additionally, deprecated timezone handling has been updated to align with Django 5 standards.

items.title: Optimize Vuex Storage to Prevent Quota Overflow in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where the Vuex localStorage quota was exceeded due to excessive task data being persisted for each app. The solution involves removing the per-app `.tasks` entries from localStorage and implementing a quota-safe storage adapter that handles `QuotaExceededError`. Users will experience smoother performance without manual localStorage clearing after the update.

items.title: Enhance localization API to track elapsed time in Platform Service ⏱️
items.summary: This update fixes the localization worker by capturing `started_at`, `finished_at`, and `elapsed` time metrics, which were previously missing from the payload sent to the PlatformGateway. As a result, the Elapsed column for Localization API rows on the usage console will now display accurate durations, aligning with other services. This change is purely additive and does not affect the translation pipeline's behavior.

items.title: Enhance Captcha Options with Cloudflare Turnstile in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces Cloudflare Turnstile as an optional captcha provider alongside the existing hCaptcha. Users can opt-in to use Turnstile by appending `?features=turnstile` to the URL, while the default behavior remains unchanged. The integration is non-breaking, ensuring existing functionalities are preserved unless users choose to enable the new feature.

items.title: Enhance Pagination and Slim Down Loading Indicator in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses two key issues in Nexior's pagination system. First, it implements an IntersectionObserver to ensure that the pagination auto-triggers when reaching the top of the scroll, enhancing user experience. Additionally, the loading indicator has been redesigned to be slimmer and more visually appealing, improving the overall interface.

items.title: Enhance API Path Handling in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a Cloudflare Worker to properly reverse-proxy authentication API paths, ensuring that requests to `/api/...`, `/oauth2/...`, and `/sso/...` are correctly routed to the auth backend. The changes prevent the frontend from misinterpreting HTML responses as JSON, which previously caused login and SSO flows to fail. Additionally, the configuration for the Worker has been updated to align with production settings.

items.title: Fix API Path Handling in Frontend Web 🌐
items.summary: This update introduces a Cloudflare Worker to reverse-proxy API paths for the PlatformFrontend, addressing issues where API calls fell through to the SPA fallback. Key changes include the addition of a new worker script and updates to the configuration file to ensure proper routing of requests. This enhancement allows the frontend to correctly handle API requests, improving functionality in the preview environment.

items.title: Enhance Connector Prompts for Clarity in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the clarity of suggestion prompts for various connectors by disambiguating names that could confuse users. For example, prompts for Google Drive and OneDrive now explicitly include their service names. This change enhances user experience by ensuring that the correct service is targeted during interactions.

items.title: Enhance Captcha Options with Cloudflare Turnstile in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces Cloudflare Turnstile as an optional captcha provider alongside the existing hCaptcha, allowing for a seamless transition without breaking changes. The implementation includes a new request field to specify the captcha provider and maintains compatibility with existing clients. Both verifiers are now managed through a unified dispatcher, ensuring consistent behavior across the application.

items.title: Migrated to npm for seamless Cloudflare Builds in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update transitions the project from Yarn to npm, aligning it with Nexior and enhancing Cloudflare Workers Builds previews. Key changes include updates to the Dockerfile, CI workflows, and README instructions, along with the removal of the yarn.lock file and the addition of a package-lock.json. This migration ensures a single lockfile for dependency management, improving build reliability.

items.title: Enhance User Interaction with New Prompt Guidance in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new partial for the aichat2 system prompt, providing guidance on when to utilize the `ask_user_question` tool. It includes a structured schema reminder and integrates the tool into the capabilities list, enhancing the model's ability to engage users effectively. The changes ensure that the model can clarify user requirements and present options more intuitively.

items.title: Enhance User Interaction with Ask User Question Card in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces the Ask User Question card to the Nexior frontend, allowing users to interactively respond to questions during conversations. The card supports single and multi-select options, a free-text fallback, and collapses to a summary after submission. Key files added include the card component and associated tests, enhancing the overall user experience and conversation flow.

items.title: Enhance User Interaction with Pause/Resume in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates the `ask_user_question` tool into the queryLoop and conversation handler, enabling a seamless pause and resume functionality. Key changes include the addition of a new `tool_results` field in the request body and enhancements to the state management of tool interactions. These improvements will enhance user experience by allowing for dynamic question prompts during conversations.

items.title: Scaffolding for User Question Feature in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the initial scaffolding for the `ask_user_question` feature, including new types and a validator to handle user input. The tool is currently inert at runtime, ensuring no changes in production until the next PR integrates it into the tool registry. Key modifications include updates to type definitions and validation rules to support the new functionality.

items.title: Streamlined Cloudflare Deploys and Toolchain Update in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces Cloudflare Workers preview deploys, allowing automatic preview URLs for each PR, enhancing the development workflow. Additionally, the project has transitioned from Yarn to npm, ensuring consistent dependency management while preserving version integrity. Key files have been updated to reflect these changes, including the removal of Yarn-specific configurations.

items.title: Restore catalog-whitelist filtering for Connection scopes in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update reinstates the catalog-whitelist filter for Connection scopes, addressing issues where Microsoft over-grants permissions on token issuance. The fix ensures that only relevant scopes are returned based on the user's consent, improving security and compliance. Additionally, new tests have been added to validate the functionality and ensure robustness.

items.title: Stabilize Default Landing Route in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the default landing route for logged-in users by replacing the unordered feature map with a stable priority array. The previous implementation caused users to be redirected to an incorrect route due to the unpredictable order of features in the database. Now, users will consistently land on the expected `/chatgpt/conversations` page, enhancing the user experience.

items.title: Automatic i18n Translation Updates in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces auto-generated translation updates for various languages based on changes from the zh-CN source. Key modifications include updates to usage.json files for Arabic, German, English, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Traditional Chinese, enhancing the application's multilingual support.

items.title: Streamline Cloudflare Workers Setup for Preview Deploys in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `wrangler.jsonc` configuration for Cloudflare Workers, enabling automatic deployment of the PlatformFrontend SPA on every push and PR. Once merged, each PR will receive a unique preview URL, enhancing the development workflow and allowing for seamless testing of changes. The setup mirrors the existing configuration used for Nexior, ensuring consistency across projects.

items.title: Enhance UI: Status Filter & Export Button Adjustments in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the layout of the status filter and export button for better usability on desktop. The status-code dropdown width has been reduced, and the export button has been widened to ensure 'Export CSV' fits on one line. Additionally, the placeholder text for the status filter has been shortened for clarity.

items.title: Enhance security by redacting passwords in Backend 🔒
items.summary: This update improves security by redacting the password value in the email/password login debug log line. Previously, the plaintext password could be exposed in logs, potentially leaking sensitive information. Now, the debug logs will show 'password=<set>' instead, ensuring better protection of user credentials.

items.title: Enhance Android Login Flow in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the Android login experience by re-running the main app initialization once the authenticated user ID is available. It introduces a monotonic run ID to prevent overlapping initialization processes, ensuring that stale requests do not interfere with the logged-in state. This change resolves issues where the app remained in a skeleton state after login until a restart.

items.title: Enhance Mobile Settings Dialog Usability in Nexior 📱
items.summary: This update improves the Settings dialog for mobile viewports by introducing a responsive design. Key changes include a new mobile flag that adjusts the dialog width to 94vw, removes the fixed sidebar, and implements a horizontal tab strip for easier navigation. The desktop layout remains unchanged, ensuring a seamless experience across devices.

items.title: Hide Mobile App Entry in User Dropdown on Native Platforms in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the 'Mobile App' entry from the user-avatar dropdown when the app is running natively on iOS or Android. The change prevents unnecessary links for users already on the native app and addresses potential App Store review concerns. The dropdown item will still appear in web builds, ensuring a seamless experience across platforms.

items.title: Streamlined Message Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update collapses the message structure into a single folded shape, resolving critical production bugs related to message retrieval and updates. Key changes include the removal of the serializer, strict validation for message formats, and a new migration script to ensure data consistency. Overall, this enhances the reliability and performance of chat interactions.

items.title: Streamline Status Code Filter for Better UI in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The status-code filter on the `/console/usages` page has been moved inline with the other filters, enhancing the layout for desktop users. This change reduces vertical space usage and improves the overall balance of the filter row. Additional adjustments include removing the redundant label and repositioning the Export button for cleaner alignment.

items.title: Fix conversation ID retention on refresh in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a bug where refreshing the chat page caused the conversation ID to disappear from the URL. The fix involves adding guards in three components to ensure that the conversation ID remains intact during page refreshes, enhancing user experience by preserving the selected chat model. Additionally, the changes prevent unnecessary resets of the active model when switching between groups.

items.title: Streamline Citation Chips by Removing Favicon in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the citation chips in chat by removing the redundant favicon, resulting in a cleaner, label-only design. Additionally, the pill's padding and height have been adjusted for improved readability. These changes not only reduce network calls but also maintain accessibility standards.

items.title: Enhance citation anchoring for chat UX in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the citation rules to ensure that citation chips are anchored to the primary entity rather than secondary attributes. The change clarifies that `<acite>` should be placed directly after the primary label of the entity, improving the accuracy of citations across various data types. This adjustment enhances the user experience by ensuring clearer and more relevant citations in chat responses.

items.title: Fix chat model display issue in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a production JavaScript crash in the chat feature by adding a guard to the model display logic in the ModelSelector component. The fix ensures that even when the model's methods are stripped during state persistence, the trigger will still render the model's name instead of throwing an error. This change improves stability and user experience when refreshing the chat page.

items.title: Refine OneDrive Scopes to Prevent Outlook Leaks in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update narrows the `Connection.scopes` for OneDrive to only include permissions from its catalog, preventing unwanted Outlook scopes from appearing. Key changes include the implementation of a new function to filter scopes before persistence, ensuring that existing connections self-heal on refresh. All tests have passed successfully, confirming the effectiveness of the fix.

items.title: Enhance Code Comment Guidelines in Index ✍️
items.summary: This update introduces a new guideline for code comments in the project, emphasizing brevity by limiting comments to three lines. The change aims to improve code readability by encouraging developers to focus on explaining the 'why' rather than the 'what'. This adjustment is based on user feedback for clearer and more concise documentation.

items.title: Fix fallback to 'credit' unit for Balance After in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where the Balance After column displayed a raw i18n key when the service was null, resulting in an incorrect display of 'service.unit.nulls'. The fix applies a fallback to the 'credit' unit in the getRemainingAmount function, ensuring that the display shows the correct localized unit. This change enhances the user experience by providing clearer information in the usage console.

items.title: Enhance SSE Resilience for Chat Engine in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update decouples the engine lifecycle from the SSE output, preventing the engine from aborting when the SSE client disconnects. Users will now receive complete messages even if they refresh the chat or experience network interruptions, ensuring accurate billing and improved user experience. The fix has been verified with all tests passing successfully.

items.title: Clamp max_turns to a constant for better control in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the `DEFAULT_MAX_TURNS` constant from 25 to 50, ensuring that the maximum turns for the agentic loop are capped effectively. The changes in `config.ts` and `handlers/conversations.ts` prevent clients from exceeding this limit, protecting the system from potential misuse. All tests have passed successfully, confirming the stability of the implementation.

items.title: Enhance Wallet Display with Balance in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update transforms the wallet icon in the top-right corner into a compact pill that displays the current balance, providing users with immediate feedback on their credits. The functionality remains unchanged, ensuring that users can still access the application picker dialog seamlessly. Additionally, the balance formatting adapts based on the amount, enhancing usability across different screen sizes.

items.title: Removed costly e2e probes for Veo and Producer in Backend 🚫
items.summary: This update removes the scheduled end-to-end probes for the Veo and Producer services, which were incurring high costs due to real API calls. The corresponding deployment files have been deleted, and the changes have been applied immediately in the cluster to halt unnecessary spending. Local testing scripts remain available for ad-hoc runs.

items.title: Fix wallet selection persistence across reloads in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses an issue where the user's wallet selection was not retained after a page reload. The fix modifies the logic to ensure that if a user has made a selection, it will be preserved even if the selected package is expired or exhausted. This change enhances user experience by preventing unwanted auto-replacements of their choices.

items.title: Fix(midjourney): wrap long prompts in task list instead of truncating with ellipsis in Nexior 🐛
